---
title: "VM Installation Guide"
execute:
  eval: false
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

## VM Installation Guide

This is the clean-install checklist for a future MS project-management VM.

## Base Packages

```bash
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y git curl rsync apache2 apache2-utils openssl sqlite3 \
  build-essential libcurl4-openssl-dev libssl-dev libxml2-dev r-base r-base-dev \
  default-jdk
```

Run the repository checker after cloning:

```bash
cd /home/<user>/BCFMS-project-management
bash scripts/check_requirements.sh
bash scripts/check_requirements.sh --install
bash scripts/check_requirements.sh --install-r
```

The R package list includes `shiny`, `shinyjs`, `DBI`, `RSQLite`, `digest`, `DT`, `rJava`, and `mailR`.

## GitHub Access

```bash
ssh -T git@github.com
```

If access fails, add the VM public key as a deploy key in the GitHub repository:

```bash
cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
```

Clone the repository:

```bash
cd /home/<user>
git clone git@github.com:yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management.git
```

## Shiny Server

```bash
sudo apt-get install -y gdebi-core
wget https://download3.rstudio.org/ubuntu-20.04/x86_64/shiny-server-1.5.23.1030-amd64.deb
sudo gdebi shiny-server-1.5.23.1030-amd64.deb
sudo systemctl enable shiny-server
sudo systemctl start shiny-server
```

Create the app folder:

```bash
sudo mkdir -p /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo chown -R shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo usermod -a -G shiny "$USER"
```

## Deploy App Files

```bash
cd /home/<user>/BCFMS-project-management
sudo rsync -av --delete --exclude 'ms_projects.db' --exclude '.Renviron' ms-app/ /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/
sudo chown -R shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
```

## Apply for https certificate

Run the following command which generates a private key 'private-key.pem' and a certificate request 'certificate-request.pem'. Change the file names to the appropriate ones.

```{bash}
cd /home/yeroslaviz/certificate_request/2027/

openssl req -newkey rsa:4096 -sha256 -nodes -keyout mscf-private-key.pem -out mscf-certificate-request.pem
```

Leave all fields at their default values.

View the content of the certificate request. You will need to insert it in the final step of the next section when submitting the request.

```{bash}
cat certificate-request.pem
```


- Login to [https://cm.harica.gr](https://cm.harica.gr) > use `Academic Login`

- Search / Select "MPI of Biochemistry", then log in with your biochem user account.

- Click under "Certificate Requests" on "Server".

Domains - `mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de`

Friendly name (optional): `mscf-vm`
Add Domains: `mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de` (unselect www)

- Product - select "For enterprise or organization (OV)" and click "Next".

- Details / Authorization Summary - check the information and confirm with "Next".

- Submit your request by selecting "Submit CSR manually" and paste the content of your "certificate-request.pem" (created in the previous section: "Create a valid certificate request and a private key").

After clicking "Submit request", your certificate request is uploaded. It is now under review, and will be approved soon.

When we get the email, that the certifiacte is ready for us, I downloaded

`PEM (Cert.pem) `and `PEM bundle (Cert_bundle.pem)` and moved them to the folder `/home/yeroslaviz/certificate_request/2027`

```
$ tree -L 2
.
├── 2027_certificate-request.pem
├── 2027_private-key.pem
├── Cert.pem
└── Cert_bundle.pem
```

It is first best to compare the certifiacte to the private key.

```bash
sudo openssl x509 \
    -in /home/yeroslaviz/certificate_request/2027/Cert_bundle.pem \
    -pubkey -noout |
    openssl pkey -pubin -outform DER |
    sha256sum

sudo openssl pkey \
    -in /home/yeroslaviz/certificate_request/2027/2027_private-key.pem \
    -pubout -outform DER |
    sha256sum

```
The has strings **must** be identical!

Creating a new folder for the new certificate:

```bash
sudo mkdir -p /etc/apache2/certificate/2027
```

Rename the two certifiactes to the new place with a new name

```bash
sudo cp Cert.pem /etc/apache2/certificate/2027/2027_mscf-vm.pem

sudo cp Cert_bundle.pem /etc/apache2/certificate/2027/2027_mscf-vm_bundle.pem


```

Copying the private key to its new place:

```bash
sudo cp 2027_private-key.pem \
    /etc/apache2/certificate/2027/2027_private-key.pem
```

Setting the permissions:

```bash
sudo chown -R root:root /etc/apache2/certificate/2027
sudo chmod 644 /etc/apache2/certificate/2027/*.pem
sudo chmod 600 /etc/apache2/certificate/2027/2027_private-key.pem
```

Inspecting the certifiacte shoing me to have three of them.

```bash
grep -c 'BEGIN CERTIFICATE' \
    /etc/apache2/certificate/2027/2027_mscf-vm_bundle.pem
```
Inspect the first certificate in each file:

The standalone PEM certificate should show mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de in its Subject Alternative Names.

```bash
openssl x509 \
    -in /etc/apache2/certificate/2027/2027_mscf-vm.pem \
    -noout -subject -issuer -dates -ext subjectAltName

openssl x509 \
    -in /etc/apache2/certificate/2027/2027_mscf-vm_bundle.pem \
    -noout -subject -issuer
```

compare the hash between certificate and private key

```bash
sudo openssl x509 \
    -in /etc/apache2/certificate/2027/2027_mscf-vm.pem \
    -pubkey -noout |
    openssl pkey -pubin -outform DER |
    sha256sum

sudo openssl pkey \
    -in /etc/apache2/certificate/2027/2027_private-key.pem \
    -pubout -outform DER |
    sha256sum
```

Change the Apache configuration

```bash
sudo nano /etc/apache2/sites-available/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf
```

Change the following section to contain the new paths to the new certificates:
(Using the actual paths directly initially avoids uncertainty about the current symlinks.)

```
<VirtualHost *:443>
    ServerName mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de

    SSLEngine on
    SSLCertificateFile /etc/apache2/certificate/2027/2027_mscf-vm_bundle.pem
    SSLCertificateKeyFile /etc/apache2/certificate/2027/2027_private-key.pem

    # Remaining configuration...
</VirtualHost>
```

Test and reload Apache

First check the configuration without disrupting the running server:

```bash
sudo a2ensite ms-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo apache2ctl configtest
```
You should get:

```
Syntax OK
```

Then reload Apache gracefully:

```bash
sudo systemctl reload apache2
```
Check its status:

```bash
sudo systemctl --no-pager --full status apache2
```

Confirm the certificate being served

```bash
echo | openssl s_client \
    -connect mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de:443 \
    -servername mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de 2>/dev/null |
    openssl x509 -noout -subject -issuer -serial -dates
```
Also verify the chain:

```bash
echo | openssl s_client \
    -connect mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de:443 \
    -servername mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de \
    -verify_return_error 2>&1 |
    tail -n 5
```
A successful result should contain:

```
Verify return code: 0 (ok)
```

Keep the existing `/etc/apache2/certificate/2027/` files unchanged until the new certificate has been verified. That gives you a simple rollback by restoring the two old paths in the Apache configuration and reloading Apache.

### Changes on 22.07.2026

[Harica](https://cm.harica.gr/) has revoked the old certifiacte. For that reason I have applied for a new certifiacate. The new one was saved under `/home/yeroslaviz/certificate_request/2027/2027_07-22-2026`.

**The older Cert files were deleted.**

``bash
cd /home/yeroslaviz/certificate_request/2027
rm 2027_certificate-request.pem 2027_private-key.pem Cert*
```

The rest was kept in the same way. I copied the new Cert files to the apache2 folder and restarted the apache2 server after confirming the hashes are identical.

The server is back online and still works with https.

## Initialize Database

```bash
cd /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo -u shiny Rscript setup_database.R
```

For schema/seed checks without deleting the DB:

```bash
sudo -u shiny MS_RESET_DB=0 Rscript setup_database.R
```

## Runtime Environment

```bash
sudo tee /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron >/dev/null <<'EOF'
AUTH_MODE=ldap
TRUST_PROXY_AUTH_USER_QUERY=1
LDAP_URI=ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de
LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
LDAP_ATTR_UID=uid
LDAP_ATTR_MAIL=mail
LDAP_ATTR_PHONE=telephoneNumber
LDAP_ATTR_OU=ou
LDAP_ATTR_CN=cn
LDAP_ATTR_GIVENNAME=givenName
LDAP_ATTR_SN=sn
MS_FACILITY_EMAIL=ms-service@biochem.mpg.de
MS_MAIL_FROM=ms-service@biochem.mpg.de
MS_POOL_FALLBACK_EMAIL=omicsdesk@biochem.mpg.de
SMTP_HOST=msx.biochem.mpg.de
SMTP_PORT=25
SMTP_SSL=0
SMTP_TLS=0
# Optional: comma-separated recipient domains, for example biochem.mpg.de.
# MS_EMAIL_ALLOWED_DOMAINS=biochem.mpg.de
MS_UPLOAD_ROOT=/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs-ms-projects
MS_MAX_UPLOAD_MB=75
EOF
sudo chown shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
sudo chmod 640 /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
```

## Apache / LDAP

Install LDAP client tooling:

```bash
sudo apt-get install -y ldap-utils libldap2-dev libsasl2-dev ca-certificates
```

Configure OpenLDAP trust:

```bash
sudo tee /etc/ldap/ldap.conf >/dev/null <<'EOF'
BASE            dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
URI             ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de
TLS_REQCERT     hard
TLS_CACERTDIR   /etc/ssl/certs
EOF
```

Copy and enable the vhost:

```bash
sudo cp /home/<user>/BCFMS-project-management/scripts/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo a2enmod ssl proxy proxy_http proxy_wstunnel headers rewrite ldap authnz_ldap
sudo a2ensite ms-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2
```

## Verification

```bash
curl -Ik https://mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de/
curl -Ik -u USER https://mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de/ms-app/
curl -I http://127.0.0.1:3838/ms-app/
```

Local maintenance login:

```bash
sudo sed -i 's/^AUTH_MODE=.*/AUTH_MODE=local/' /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
```

Return to LDAP:

```bash
sudo sed -i 's/^AUTH_MODE=.*/AUTH_MODE=ldap/' /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
```

## Backup / Restore

Backup:

```bash
sudo rsync -av /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ /srv/shiny-server/ms-app.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)/
sudo cp /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db.bak.$(date +%F-%H%M%S)
```

Restore:

```bash
sudo systemctl stop shiny-server
sudo rsync -av --delete /srv/shiny-server/ms-app.backup_YYYYMMDD_HHMM/ /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/
sudo chown -R shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo systemctl start shiny-server
```
